nodes.label: AI as diary planning assistant
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI to help me with diary planning by outlining my availability and the tasks

nodes.label: AI as report writing aid for rephrasing
nodes.label_negative: Writing reports entirely without AI assistance
nodes.grounding_span: when I'm writing reports, I find that I can't quite phrase things effectively, so I'll often put in the part I'm struggling with and ask it to improve it

nodes.label: AI for creating tuition resources
nodes.grounding_span: If I'm creating resources to use in my tuition sessions, I tend to create those myself

nodes.label: AI as collaborative brainstorming partner
nodes.grounding_span: there are times when I use it collaboratively to help me explore ideas

nodes.label: Minimal AI use in report writing to maintain individualization
nodes.label_negative: Heavy AI reliance in report writing
nodes.grounding_span: there's an unspoken expectation that if AI is used, it should only be used minimally

nodes.label: AI as virtual assistant for task automation
nodes.grounding_span: I'd like to explore how AI can automate tasks in my current business to free up time

nodes.label: Efficiency and time optimization
nodes.grounding_span: help me plan my day the most effectively

nodes.label: Clarity and accessibility in communication
nodes.grounding_span: keep the language simple enough that a non-expert could understand it

nodes.label: Personalized and individualized output
nodes.grounding_span: each report is individual to the person I'm working with

nodes.label: Creative exploration and idea generation
nodes.grounding_span: It helped me to brainstorm and narrow down what I could create

nodes.label: Autonomy and skill preservation
nodes.grounding_span: I didn't really feel inspired to continue with the course

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for rephrasing reports
nodes.grounding_span: AI helped me to rephrase it in a way that was coherent but accessible

nodes.label: Frustration with AI for creating tuition resources
nodes.grounding_span: the alphabet arc example that I shared above made me feel really frustrated

nodes.label: Openness to future AI use for automation and creativity
nodes.grounding_span: I'd like to explore how AI can automate tasks in my current business

nodes.label: Task decomposition into small, manageable chunks
nodes.grounding_span: I just take short sections and ask for help

nodes.label: Preference for minimal AI intervention to preserve individuality
nodes.grounding_span: if AI is used, it should only be used minimally
